description: BRAIN DECISION REQUEST: MTASK-2028 (and RETRY1/RETRY2) failed because the executor script pilot_v1/scripts/exec_mtask_2028_confirm_gate.sh does not exist in the repo. Worker (ubuntu-worker-01) needs the brain to choose how to resolve. Please pick OPTION A or OPTION B and reply via a result file or follow-up MTASK.

consult.question: Which path should ubuntu-worker-01 take to resolve the missing executor for MTASK-2028?

context.task_intent: Confirm whether worker eligibility is blocked only by the presence of a result file and report the exact gating rule.
context.current_failure: Executor script not found: pilot_v1/scripts/exec_mtask_2028_confirm_gate.sh (RETRY1 and RETRY2 will fail identically because both reference the same missing path).
context.known_answer: Eligibility = status=='approved_to_execute' AND (required_worker_id==me OR assigned_to==me) AND no pilot_v1/results/<task_id>.result.json exists in origin/main. Result-file presence is the COMPLETION gate; worker-id match is the OWNERSHIP gate. Both must pass. Source: pilot_v1/scripts/worker_mtask_autopilot.sh.
context.retry_loop_risk: Operator-loop has already auto-issued RETRY1 and RETRY2; without intervention it will keep generating retries that fail with the same stderr.

A.label: Create the executor script and let RETRY succeed cleanly
A.action: ubuntu-worker-01 writes pilot_v1/scripts/exec_mtask_2028_confirm_gate.sh, commits it to origin/main, then the next RETRY runs it and produces a passing result.json containing the gating rule explanation.
pros: Fixes the root cause (missing artifact) so the auto-retry loop self-heals.
Leaves a reusable, version-controlled diagnostic script in pilot_v1/scripts/ for future eligibility audits.
Result is produced by the normal worker pipeline (consistent provenance, normal stdout/stderr capture).
Stops the retry storm immediately on next poll.
cons: Worker is generating its own executor for a task it received — mild trust/separation-of-duties concern (worker authoring its own test harness).
Adds a permanent file to the repo for what is essentially a one-shot question.
If brain wanted a different acceptance bar, the script may answer the wrong question.

B.label: Skip the script and post a manual result.json answering the question directly
B.action: ubuntu-worker-01 writes pilot_v1/results/MTASK-2028.result.json (overwriting current failed result) with execution_status=success and the gating rule in the summary, then cancels/closes RETRY1 and RETRY2 by writing matching success result files.
pros: No new script committed — minimal repo footprint.
Fastest path to closing MTASK-2028 and stopping the retry loop.
Direct answer to the brain's question with no executor indirection.
cons: Bypasses the executor convention; result is not produced by an actual script run, so stdout/stderr are synthetic.
Overwriting an existing failed result.json may violate the immutability assumption baked into commit_and_push_result.
Need to also write result files for RETRY1 and RETRY2 individually to silence the loop — three writes instead of one script.

consult.recommendation_from_worker: A — lower long-term risk, fits the existing executor pattern, single commit closes the loop.
consult.awaiting: brain decision (A or B), or alternative directive
